누가복음 21:1.content: He looked up and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.

누가복음 21:2.content: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.

누가복음 21:3.content: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,

누가복음 21:4.content: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on.”

누가복음 21:5.content: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,

누가복음 21:6.content: “As for these things which you see, the days will come, in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down.”

누가복음 21:7.content: They asked him, “Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?”

누가복음 21:8.content: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them.

누가복음 21:9.content: When you hear of wars and disturbances, don’t be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won’t come immediately.”

누가복음 21:10.content: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

누가복음 21:11.content: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.

누가복음 21:12.content: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.

누가복음 21:13.content: It will turn out as a testimony for you.

누가복음 21:14.content: Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,

누가복음 21:15.content: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.

누가복음 21:16.content: You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will cause some of you to be put to death.

누가복음 21:17.content: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake.

누가복음 21:18.content: And not a hair of your head will perish.

누가복음 21:19.content: “By your endurance you will win your lives.

누가복음 21:20.content: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.

누가복음 21:21.content: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the middle of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.

누가복음 21:22.content: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

누가복음 21:23.content: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.

누가복음 21:24.content: They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

누가복음 21:25.content: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;

누가복음 21:26.content: men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

누가복음 21:27.content: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

누가복음 21:28.content: But when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”

누가복음 21:29.content: He told them a parable. “See the fig tree and all the trees.

누가복음 21:30.content: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.

누가복음 21:31.content: Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that God’s Kingdom is near.

누가복음 21:32.content: Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.

누가복음 21:33.content: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.

누가복음 21:34.content: “So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.

누가복음 21:35.content: For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.

누가복음 21:36.content: Therefore be watchful all the time, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”

누가복음 21:37.content: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.

누가복음 21:38.content: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.
